Argentina's President Javier Milei speaks after the La Libertad Avanza party won the midterm election, which is seen as crucial for Milei's administration after U.S.
BUENOS AIRES, Dec 26 (Reuters) - (This December 26 story has been corrected to change the peso amount from billion to trillion in paragraph 2)
Argentina's Congress passed the 2026 budget on Friday, the first approved by legislators since President Javier Milei took office in late 2023.
The budget, passed 46 votes to 25 with one abstention, includes spending of 148 trillion pesos ($102 billion) and projects South America's second-biggest economy will grow 5% with inflation at 10.1%."},{"url":"https://buenosairesherald.com/politics/argentine-senators-debate-mileis-2026-budget-bill","title":"Argentine senators approve 2026 budget in first for Milei - Buenos Aires Herald","content":"Dec 26, 2025 — The budget estimates economic growth of 5% for 2026, annual inflation of 10.1%, and a dollar exchange rate of around AR$1,423 in December next ...Read more
Argentine senators approve 2026 budget in first for Milei - Buenos Aires Herald
Argentina’s Senate has voted to pass President Javier Milei’s 2026 budget, his first since taking office.
Shortly after 9 p.m. on Friday, senators approved the budget bill with 46 in favor, 25 against, and one abstention.
The text approved by the senate was the same as the text approved in the lower house last week."},{"url":"https://derechadiario.com.ar/us/argentina/president-javier-milei-presented-the-2026-budget-on-national-television","title":"President Javier Milei presented the 2026 Budget in a nationwide broadcast | La Derecha Diario en Inglés","content":"President Javier Milei spoke on Monday in a national broadcast to present the 2026 Budget that he sent to the National Congress, in a historic event that consolidated the path of fiscal order and growth with freedom.
The message was recorded in the White Hall of the Casa Rosada during the afternoon, accompanied by the Minister of Economy, Luis Caputo, the presidential spokesperson, Manuel Adorni, and the deputy José Luis Espert.
The President stressed that this budget was "the first in Argentine history to include the Reciprocal Obligations Extinction Regime, to continue rebuilding the relationship between the national government and the provinces".
The head of state maintained that the commitment to deep reforms required working "side by side with governors, deputies, and senators who want a different Argentina".
The President also detailed the increases planned in the budget: 5% in real terms for pensions, 17% in health, 8% in education, 5% for disability pensions, and an increase of 4.8 trillion pesos (4.8 trillion pesos) for national universities."},{"url":"https://ground.news/article/budget-2026-in-a-wink-to-the-provinces-milei-strengthens-the-retirement-funds","title":"Budget 2026: in a Wink to the Provinces, Milei Strengthens ...","date":"1 day ago","content":"1 day ago — President Javier Milei formalized a restructuring of the General Budget of the National Administration for the financial year 2026."}]}

Javier Milei's party made significant gains in the latest midterm elections, giving it more sway in CongressImage: Lucio Tavora/Xinhua News Agency/picture alliance. Argentina's Congress approved on Friday the 2026 budget suggested by President Javier Milei, a first since he was elected president over two years ago. Previously, Milei's government extended the 2023 budget without passing a bill in Congress, resulting in inflation across sectors. \"We are not going to spend more than we make, we are going to get our accounts in order,\" said Senator Ezequiel Atauche, a member of Milei's party. Milei's party, La Libertad Avanza, made significant gains in the newly elected Congress, with the government hoping this will help in pushing forward its reforms to Argentina's labor and tax systems.
